questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.3]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\coordinate (R) at (9,0);\coordinate (Q) at (9,12);\coordinate (P) at (-6.4,4.8);\draw (O)--(P) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$8$};\draw (P)--(Q) node[midway,above,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (Q)--(R) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$12$};\draw (R)--(O) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$9$};\draw (O)--(Q) node[midway,below right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$y$};\draw (8.5,0)--(8.5,0.5)--(9,0.5);\coordinate (va) at ($(O)!0.5cm!(P)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(O)!0.5cm!(Q)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(O)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
All lengths are in centimetres. Find $x$ by first finding the intermediate side $y$.

options.why: Gave the intermediate side $y$ as the answer, forgetting the second step.
Added the two sides $8$ and $15$ in the second step without squaring them.
Subtracted the squares in the second step instead of adding them.

questions.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
$y=\sqrt{9^{2}+12^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{225}$
$=15$
2. **Find the required side**
$x=\sqrt{8^{2}+15^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{289}$
$=17$

questions.question_text: A first right-angled triangle has shorter sides $4\text{ cm}$ and $7\text{ cm}$. Its hypotenuse is one side of a second right-angled triangle whose other side is $9\text{ cm}$. Find the hypotenuse of the second triangle. Round to $2$ decimal places.

options.why: Rounded the intermediate side before the second step instead of only at the end.
Stopped after the first step and gave the intermediate side.
Subtracted the squares in the second step instead of adding them.

questions.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
$\sqrt{4^{2}+7^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{65}$
2. **Find the required side**
$\sqrt{9^{2}+(\sqrt{65})^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{81+65}$
$=\sqrt{146}$
$\approx12.08\text{ cm}$

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.4]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (M) at (5,0);\coordinate (B) at (14,0);\coordinate (T) at (5,6);\draw (A)--(T) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (T)--(B) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$y$};\draw[dashed] (T)--(M) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$6\text{ cm}$};\draw (A)--(M) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$5\text{ cm}$};\draw (M)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$9\text{ cm}$};\draw (5,0.5)--(5.5,0.5)--(5.5,0);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
This triangle is split into two right-angled triangles by its perpendicular height of $6\text{ cm}$. Find its perimeter. Round to $2$ decimal places.

options.why: Added only the two slant sides and left out the base.
Used the vertical height as each slant side instead of calculating them.
Correct value but written in metres instead of centimetres.

questions.solution_text:
$x=\sqrt{5^{2}+6^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{61}$
$y=\sqrt{9^{2}+6^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{117}$
The perimeter adds the two slant sides and the full base.
$P=\sqrt{61}+\sqrt{117}+14$
$\approx32.63\text{ cm}$

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.9]\draw (0,0) circle (2.5);\fill (0,0) circle (1.5pt);\draw (0,0)--(2,-1.5) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$12.5\text{ cm}$};\draw[dashed] (0,0)--(0,-1.5);\draw (-2,-1.5)--(2,-1.5);\node[above] at (-0.9,-1.5) {$20\text{ cm}$};\draw (0.25,-1.5)--(0.25,-1.25)--(0,-1.25);\draw[dashed] (0,-1.5)--(0,-2.5);\node[right] at (0.05,-2.0) {$d$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A cylindrical pipe of radius $12.5\text{ cm}$ holds water with a flat surface $20\text{ cm}$ wide. Find the depth $d$ of the water.

options.why: Gave the distance from the centre to the surface and forgot to subtract it from the radius.
Subtracted the half-width from the radius without using Pythagoras.
Added the intermediate length to the radius instead of subtracting it.

questions.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
Half the surface is $10\text{ cm}$, forming a right-angled triangle with the radius.
$\sqrt{12.5^{2}-10^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{56.25}$
$=7.5\text{ cm}$
The depth is the radius minus this length.
$d=12.5-7.5$
$=5\text{ cm}$

questions.question_text: A first right-angled triangle has shorter sides $5\text{ cm}$ and $8\text{ cm}$. Its hypotenuse $x$ is one side of a second right-angled triangle whose hypotenuse is $13\text{ cm}$. Find the third side $y$ of the second triangle in exact form.

options.why: Added the squares in the second step instead of subtracting them.
Gave the intermediate side $x$ instead of continuing to the second step.
Subtracted the original side $8$ instead of the intermediate hypotenuse.

questions.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
$x=\sqrt{5^{2}+8^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{89}\text{ cm}$
2. **Find the required side**
$y=\sqrt{13^{2}-(\sqrt{89})^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{169-89}$
$=\sqrt{80}\text{ cm}$
